front: What is an unbalanced dataset in machine learning?
back: An unbalanced dataset is one where the distribution of examples across the classes is not equal. Often, one class (the majority class) has significantly more examples than another class (the minority class).

front: Why is accuracy a misleading metric for evaluating models on unbalanced datasets?
back: Accuracy can be very high by simply predicting the majority class every time. For example, a model with 99% accuracy is useless if it always predicts 'not fraud' on a dataset where 99% of transactions are not fraudulent and 1% are fraudulent.

front: What are two common techniques for handling unbalanced datasets at the data level?
back:
1. Oversampling: Increasing the number of instances in the minority class (e.g., SMOTE - Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique).
2. Undersampling: Reducing the number of instances in the majority class.

front: What metrics are more appropriate than accuracy for evaluating model performance on unbalanced datasets?
back: Precision, Recall (Sensitivity), F1-Score, and the Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC-ROC). These metrics provide a better view of how well the model performs on the minority class.

front: What is a cost-sensitive learning approach to handling unbalanced datasets?
back: An algorithm-level technique where a higher cost is assigned to misclassifying examples from the minority class. This forces the model to pay more attention to correctly predicting the underrepresented class during training.
